title: 6 Managing ICBC Agents芒聙聶 Account Dishonoured PADs, Account Errors, and Misdirected Chargebacks
body_text:
6	Managing ICBC Agents芒聙聶 Account Dishonoured PADs, Account Errors, and Misdirected Chargebacks
6.1	Managing Dishonoured Pre-Authorized Payments (PADS) for ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Deposit Accounts
Central 1 creates PADs (the sweep) to debit ICBC agents芒聙聶 accounts and credit ICBC. If an ICBC agent芒聙聶s PAD is dishonoured, contact Central 1 Payment Services Specialists immediately. See Section 1.2, Contacts and Websites, for contact details. PADs must not be returned through the clearing.
6.2	Managing Deposit Errors for an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account
ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account Deposit Errors Identified by ICBC
If ICBC identifies an error (e.g., a double posting, an incorrect amount, a wrong agent number), they will request an adjustment from Central 1. Central 1 creates an AFT transaction and the adjustment appears on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD) report within two business days. Authorized users can access adjustment notices on the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for more information.
ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account Deposit Errors Identified by your Financial Institution
If you discover an error (e.g., a PAD was processed for the wrong amount, or duplicate ICBC Deposit Slip), you must submit a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request in ServiceNow. Central 1 will investigate the deposit. If Central 1 determines that an adjustment is required, an AFT transaction is initiated to the appropriate ICBC agent芒聙聶s account. The adjustment appears on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD) report within two business days of processing the adjustment. Financial institutions can access adjustment notices on the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for more information.
Note: 	You can芒聙聶t charge overdraft fees to the ICBC agent芒聙聶s account if an error caused a negative account balance. See Section 3.4, Reimbursement for an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account Overdraft Charges, for more information.

Your financial institution is responsible for clerical errors made during deposits to the ICBC agent芒聙聶s account and when submitting an ICBC Deposit Slip in ServiceNow. You are also responsible for monitoring an ICBC agent芒聙聶s deposit account to ensure it芒聙聶s at a zero balance each day and that all adjustments are processed. If an adjustment wasn芒聙聶t posted to the account, request an update on the open ServiceNow ticket or contact Central 1芒聙聶s Payment Services Specialists to determine if an adjustment is pending. See Section 1.2, Contacts and Websites, for contact details.
6.3	Managing Chargebacks for an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account
Cheques included in an ICBC agent芒聙聶s account must be returned to Royal Bank (RBC) for processing if dishonoured. To ensure dishonoured items are returned to RBC and not to your financial institution, you must enter the RBC transit number (09150-003) in the return information fields when processing ICBC agent deposit cheques in Branch Capture. See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on entering return information.
If you receive a misdirected chargeback for an ICBC agent account, submit a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request in ServiceNow to request Central 1 to redirect the item to RBC.
Create a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request in ServiceNow as follows:
Go to
